Forms have always been one of the most important parts of Angular applications. However, traditional Reactive Forms often require subscriptions, manual synchronization, FormArray management, and extra boilerplate to keep everything aligned.
With Angular 22, Signal Forms introduce a new way of thinking.
Instead of treating the form as the source of truth, Signal Forms make your application state the source of truth and let the form derive from it.
The result is simpler code, fewer bugs, and a more reactive development experience.
What Are Signal Forms?
Signal Forms bring Angular Signals directly into form management.
Rather than creating a FormGroup and synchronizing values between component state and form controls, your model itself becomes reactive.
import { signal } from '@angular/core';const profileState = signal({
fullName: '',
contactEmail: ''
});
Your form is generated from this reactive state and stays synchronized automatically.
This creates a single reactive model throughout the application.
Why Signal Forms Matter
